name: Irish Soda Bread#2 Monica Sheridan
recipeInstructions:
1. Rub the butter into the flour using a fork, mixing until well combined.
2. Add the salt and baking soda, stirring to incorporate all dry ingredients evenly.
3. Gradually add the buttermilk (or sweet milk) and stir with a wooden spoon until a soft dough forms.
4. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead gently by hand until smooth.
5. Shape the dough into a round ball, about 1-1/2 inches thick.
6. Make an X in the center of the dough using a floured knife.
7. Bake at 425°F (220°C) for 30 to 35 minutes.
From The art of Irish cooking by Monica Sheridan
